name: Chieftain
concept: The tribe is yours to lead, which means it is yours to keep alive. Every mouth, every cub, every quarrel and hunt and hard winter comes home to you. You did not ask to carry them all, or perhaps you fought for the right. Either way the lodge is yours now, and the weight of it never sets down.
duty: Lead, and they live; falter, and they scatter or starve. The tribe's fate is the measure of you, and it is never off your shoulders.

look.label: Look
options: worn by the weight of years

look.label: Sign of office
options: the tribe's trust, and nothing more
a circlet of bone and leaf

gear.label: Office
options: the tribe's stores in your keeping
a token every elf here respects

gear.label: Burden
options: a hard decision you've not yet made
a rival who wants the lodge
the names of everyone the winters have taken

moves.name: Hold Them Together
moves.trigger: When you rally, command, or steady the tribe in a hard moment.

results.success: They follow your lead, and hold.
results.failure: They waver, split, or defy you, and the GM shows you the cost.
advantage: Hold 1 Resolve (spend: hearten them for a boost die on what comes / cut to the truth of a quarrel / rouse them to act as one body / quell a panic before it spreads).
You feel exactly where the tribe's fear or anger truly sits.
threat: Someone resents the order, and will remember.
Holding them costs you: a favor spent, a doubt sown, your own strength drained.
results.triumph: They would follow you into the long-cold itself.
results.despair: The tribe fractures, or turns against you, at the worst possible time.

hold.name: Resolve
spend: Hearten the tribe: they take a boost die on what comes next.
Cut through a quarrel to the truth beneath it.
Rouse them to move and act as one body.
Quell a rising panic before it spreads.

moves.name: The Tribe Looks to You
moves.trigger: Because every life in the Holt is yours to answer for:
moves.text: When you spend yourself for the tribe — take the hard watch, the hard choice, the blame — they trust you the more for it, and rally to you when it counts. But you carry them always; their losses are your wounds, and you cannot set the weight down while you hold the lodge.

moves.name: Judge
moves.trigger: When you weigh a dispute, a claim, or a person's word before the tribe.

results.success: Ask the GM one: who here speaks true? what does each truly want? what will heal this, and what will worsen it?
results.failure: You misjudge it, and your ruling makes the wound deeper.
advantage: Your judgment satisfies even the side that lost.
threat: Whatever you decide, someone will carry a grudge for it.
results.triumph: You render a judgment the whole tribe knows to be just.
results.despair: You rule badly on something that matters, and it festers.

moves.name: War-Leader
moves.trigger: When you lead the tribe in a fight or a defense of the Holt.

results.success: The tribe fights well under your hand; the line holds or the charge lands.
results.failure: The plan breaks, or holding costs the tribe dearly.
advantage: Place your fighters where they will do the most and lose the least.
threat: A gain here leaves a gap there.
The fight goes longer and harder than it should.
results.triumph: You win the day with the tribe whole.
results.despair: The fight turns to disaster, and elves you knew do not come home.

moves.name: Speak for the Tribe
moves.trigger: When you parley with outsiders — another tribe, a human, a troll — as the tribe's voice.

results.success: You reach terms the tribe can live with.
results.failure: The talk fails, or the terms cost more than you can pay.
advantage: You gain a small edge — a favor, a truce, a warning of their intent.
They come away respecting the Holt.
threat: The terms bind you tighter than you'd like.
One side leaves looking for a reason to break faith.
results.triumph: You forge an alliance or a peace that changes the Holt's fortunes.
results.despair: The parley turns to insult or bloodshed, and you carry it home.

bonds: ____ stood by me when I took the lodge, and I trust their counsel above all.
____ thinks they should lead instead, and they may not be wrong.
I carry a decision that will hurt ____, and I have not yet found the courage to make it.
____ reminds me why the weight is worth carrying.
I failed ____ once, when I led, and I have sworn it will not happen again.
communityTie: The Hollow-Oak Holt is mine to keep alive; ____ is the one who reminds me I do not carry it alone.
When the hard winters come, the whole tribe turns to me, and I turn quietly to ____.
Eldest Marrowen counsels me, but ____ is the one who tells me the truths no one else will.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Resolve by 1.
Name a second: choose an elf who can lead in your stead, and lean on them.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Bind an old wound: heal a rift in the tribe that has festered for years.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Lay down the lodge: pass leadership on your own terms, resolving the fallout in play, and become an elder.
Unite the tribes: forge a greater people from many holts, founding a Bastion (a gathering-place) that outlasts you.
